transportedTraceSpace.name: X_aug
transportedTraceSpace.definition: closure Ran(R_aug) with quotient norm inf{||f||_V:R_aug f=x}

finiteAugmentedRepairInputStatus.label: finite augmented repair input
finiteAugmentedRepairInputStatus.reason: The finite theorem supplies D_aug,N>=0, K_N+R_aug,N^*D_aug,N R_aug,N>=0, and Mu annihilation on ker R_aug,N.

traceConvergenceInputStatus.label: graph-dual trace convergence
traceConvergenceInputStatus.reason: R_aug,N converges strongly to R_aug in the transported graph-dual trace topology, and the kernel entries converge compact-open.

klmPositiveTailInputStatus.label: KLM positive Volterra tail
klmPositiveTailInputStatus.reason: The all-omega Weyl/KLM theorem makes the diagonal Volterra tail nonnegative on the completed form domain.

greenClosureInputStatus.label: closed Green/Mellin form domain
greenClosureInputStatus.reason: The Green-lift closure theorem supplies density, form continuity, and closed trace-kernel compatibility.

boundedNonnegativeRepairStatus.label: bounded nonnegative transported repair
boundedNonnegativeRepairStatus.reason: The finite positive repairs are compatible with the graph-dual trace convergence.  Lower semicontinuity of closed nonnegative forms and the quotient norm on X_aug give a bounded nonnegative limit repair D_aug.

closedRepairedAugmentedFormStatus.label: closed repaired augmented form
closedRepairedAugmentedFormStatus.reason: K+R_aug^*D_aug R_aug is the closed-form limit of nonnegative finite repaired forms, hence is nonnegative on the completed augmented trace-fiber domain.

proof: Use the quotient norm on X_aug to identify trace-side forms with forms on V modulo ker R_aug.
Use R_aug,N -> R_aug to transport finite trace-image forms to X_aug.
Use finite D_aug,N>=0 and lower semicontinuity to obtain D_aug>=0.
Use KLM positivity for the Volterra tail and Green closure for the completed form domain.
